reason: Backend guidance entry point for runtime extraction, SQLite lifecycle, Windows integration boundaries, logging, and backend quality conventions.
Frontend guidance entry point for platform ports, state isolation, type safety, hooks, and frontend quality conventions.
Confirmed product scope, compatibility requirements, stable acceptance criteria, and explicit non-goals inherited by every implementation child.
Authoritative desktop architecture, active repository and candidate transition model, generation boundaries, OperationCoordinator responsibilities, transport contracts, and update transaction design.
Approved 14-child dependency DAG, per-child start and rollback gates, feasibility/lock/journal/VM prerequisites, and exact AC-to-DES-to-child-to-validation/evidence trace matrix.
Accepted framework and product-delivery decision that constrains the desktop implementation boundary.
Accepted hybrid transport decision, independent Wails feasibility gate, continuous ACK and out-of-order event handling, and bounded backpressure contract.
Accepted NSIS launcher transaction, independent handoff feasibility gate, anti-downgrade rules, rollback boundary, and data-format fence.
Observed Web, PWA, Docker, API, database, scheduler, streaming, and test compatibility surfaces that implementation must preserve.
Pinned Wails tuple, official API and source evidence, AssetServer Flusher limitation, platform constraints, and NSIS updater boundary that motivate the design and feasibility gates.
